title: Classic Spaghetti Carbonara
summary: A traditional Italian pasta dish made with eggs, cheese, pancetta, and black pepper. Simple yet incredibly flavorful.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to boil and cook the spaghetti according to package directions until al dente.
While pasta cooks, fry the pancetta in a large pan over medium heat until crispy, about 8-10 minutes. Remove from heat.
In a bowl, whisk together the eggs, Pecorino Romano, Parmesan, and a generous amount of black pepper.
Reserve 1 cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the spaghetti.
Add the hot pasta to the pan with pancetta, tossing to coat. Remove from heat.
Pour the egg mixture over the pasta, tossing quickly and continuously to create a creamy sauce. Add reserved pasta water as needed to achieve desired consistency.
Serve immediately with extra cheese and black pepper on top.
tips: The key is to toss the pasta off the heat to avoid scrambling the eggs
Use freshly grated cheese for the best flavor and texture

title: Homemade Margherita Pizza
summary: The queen of pizzas! Fresh mozzarella, San Marzano tomatoes, and basil on a perfectly crispy crust.
instructions: Preheat your oven to the highest temperature (typically 475-500°F) with a pizza stone inside for at least 30 minutes.
Mix the crushed tomatoes with minced garlic, 1 tablespoon olive oil, and salt. Set aside.
Divide the dough into two portions. On a floured surface, stretch one portion into a 12-inch circle.
Transfer to a pizza peel dusted with semolina flour.
Spread half the tomato sauce over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border.
Distribute half the mozzarella pieces evenly over the sauce.
Slide the pizza onto the preheated stone and bake for 10-12 minutes until the crust is golden and cheese is bubbly.
Remove from oven, drizzle with remaining olive oil, and top with fresh basil leaves.
Repeat with remaining dough and toppings for the second pizza.
tips: A pizza stone creates a crispier crust than a baking sheet
Don't overload the pizza with toppings or it won't cook properly

title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
summary: Soft, chewy, and loaded with chocolate chips. These classic cookies are perfect for any occasion!
instructions: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
In a large bowl, cream together the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar until light and fluffy, about 3-4 minutes.
Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla extract.
Gradually blend in the flour mixture until just combined.
Fold in the chocolate chips with a spatula or wooden spoon.
Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
Bake for 9-11 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown but centers still look slightly underdone.
Let cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
tips: Slightly underbaking ensures soft, chewy cookies
Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies

title: Thai Green Curry
summary: A fragrant and spicy Thai curry with coconut milk, vegetables, and your choice of protein. Authentic and delicious!
ingredients: 500g chicken breast, sliced (or tofu for vegetarian)
1 tbsp palm sugar or brown sugar
Fresh basil and cilantro for garnish
instructions: Heat the oil in a large wok or deep pan over medium-high heat.
Add the curry paste and stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in half the coconut milk and stir well to combine with the curry paste.
Add the chicken pieces and cook for 5 minutes until nearly cooked through.
Add the remaining coconut milk and stock, then bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the bell pepper, green beans, eggplant, and kaffir lime leaves. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Stir in the fish sauce and sugar, adjusting to taste.
Garnish with fresh basil and cilantro. Serve hot with jasmine rice and lime wedges.
tips: Add more curry paste for extra heat, or coconut milk to make it milder
Fresh kaffir lime leaves add authentic flavor but can be omitted if unavailable

title: Caesar Salad
summary: Crisp romaine lettuce with a creamy, garlicky dressing, Parmesan cheese, and crunchy croutons. A timeless classic!
ingredients: ¾ cup extra virgin olive oil
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: To make the dressing, combine garlic, anchovies, egg yolks, Dijon mustard, lemon juice, and Worcestershire sauce in a bowl.
Whisk vigorously while slowly drizzling in the olive oil until the dressing is thick and emulsified.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to let flavors meld.
Place the chopped romaine lettuce in a large salad bowl.
Pour the dressing over the lettuce and toss until every leaf is well coated.
Add the croutons and toss again gently.
Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese.
Serve immediately for the best texture.
tips: For a safer option, use pasteurized eggs in the dressing
Make your own croutons by toasting cubed bread with olive oil and garlic

title: Beef Tacos
summary: Flavorful seasoned ground beef in crispy taco shells, topped with fresh ingredients. Perfect for Taco Tuesday!
ingredients: Salsa and hot sauce to taste
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 6-8 minutes.
Add the diced onion and garlic to the beef and cook for another 3-4 minutes until softened.
Drain excess fat from the pan.
Stir in the taco seasoning and water. Simmer for 5 minutes until the sauce thickens.
While the meat simmers, warm the taco shells according to package directions.
Fill each taco shell with the seasoned beef.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, cheese, sour cream, and salsa.
Garnish with fresh cilantro if desired and serve immediately.
tips: Make your own taco seasoning with chili powder, cumin, paprika, and garlic powder
Try grilling your tortillas for extra flavor
